new_classes.label_en: PGI Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22042928/22042979/22042980)
new_classes.label_de: g.g.A.-Stillwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22042928/22042979/22042980)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-sparkling wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheadings 2204 29 79 and 2204 29 80 (Chapter 22).

new_classes.label_en: PGI Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres with High Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042979)
new_classes.label_de: g.g.A.-Stillwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern mit hohem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042979)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-sparkling wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of more than 2 litres, with a high actual alcoholic strength, classified under CN subheading 2204 29 79 (Chapter 22). See additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 22042979: (1) PGI status (via hasGeographicOriginQuality someValuesFrom ProtectedGeographicalIndication), (2) container volume > 2 litres (via maxContainerVolumeL minExclusive 2), (3) still wine (isCarbonated hasValue false), and (4) the CN code string anchor for the high-alcohol subtype. The legal text references 'additional note 6(a) to this chapter', which defines the specific alcoholic strength threshold that distinguishes 22042979 from its sibling 22042980. Because the current TBox does not contain a property for 'actual alcoholic strength' that distinguishes the high/low split within the large-container PGI group (the existing alcoholByVolumePercent property is present but no explicit threshold value for the 22042979 vs 22042980 split is modelled), the alcoholic strength discriminator is not fully axiomatised. A coverage deduction of ~0.28 reflects this gap.
